title: The Normal Distribution
description: The normal distribution as a continuous probability model for data that clusters symmetrically around a mean; standardising to Z and using the standard normal distribution to find probabilities and unknown values.
objectives: Describe the normal distribution N(mu, sigma^2) as a symmetric, bell-shaped continuous probability model
Standardise a normal variable using Z = (X - mu) / sigma to obtain a standard normal Z ~ N(0,1)
Use the standard normal CDF Phi(z) to find P(X < a), P(X > a) and P(a < X < b) for a given normal distribution
Find an unknown value a from a given probability using the inverse normal method
Approximate a binomial distribution B(n,p) by a normal distribution for large n, applying a continuity correction

lessons.title: Inverse normal, and approximating a binomial
